title: Refactor paste module into platform backends

context.current_state: src-tauri/src/paste.rs is single file, hardcodes xdotool calls.
context.problem: No place to hang Windows impl; cfg-gate inside one file would be unreadable.
context.goal: Split into module folder with per-OS files. Public API unchanged so lib.rs callers compile untouched.

public_api.signature: pub fn type_text(text: &str, target_window: Option<&str>) -> anyhow::Result<()>

steps: Create src-tauri/src/paste/mod.rs with cfg(target_os) re-exports of active_window_id + type_text from platform submodules
Create src-tauri/src/paste/linux.rs containing existing xdotool implementation verbatim (move, do not rewrite)
Create src-tauri/src/paste/windows.rs as a stub: both fns return None / Err(anyhow!("not implemented")) with todo!() forbidden — must compile and link
Verify lib.rs line `mod paste;` still resolves (Rust auto-detects paste/mod.rs)
Run `cargo check --target x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu` and (if cross toolchain available) `cargo check --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc`
acceptance: cargo check passes on Linux with no warnings about unused imports
Existing dictation flow on Linux still works end-to-end (regress test)
Stub windows.rs file exists and is referenced by mod.rs
